cases.title: Participant signs in with email and password via POST /auth/login
preconditions: API server running at /api/v1
Existing participant account registered with known email and password
No Authorization header required (public endpoint)
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with { email, password } matching the registered account
Inspect HTTP status and response body
cases.expected: HTTP 200; response includes JWT token and profile with userId, email, displayName, and roles array; satisfies FR-33 credential-based sign-in with authenticated JWT session

cases.title: Login-issued JWT authenticates GET /me for current actor
preconditions: Existing user account with known credentials
Auth module and session middleware available
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid { email, password }
GET /api/v1/me with Authorization: Bearer {token} from login response
cases.expected: GET /me returns HTTP 200 with profile matching login response (userId, email, role/roles); login JWT establishes authenticated session usable on protected endpoints

cases.title: Browser sign-in page issues JWT and enables protected navigation
preconditions: Web app running with dedicated /login route
Fresh browser context with no stored JWT
Existing account with known email and password
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter email and password and submit the form
Observe post-submit navigation and TopBar account control
Navigate to a protected participant route
cases.expected: Credentials validated; JWT stored client-side per Flow 0b; user redirected to intended route or default landing; TopBar account button shows display name; protected routes accessible without redirect to /login

cases.title: POST /auth/login succeeds without prior authentication
preconditions: Existing account with known credentials
Request sent with no Authorization bearer header
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid { email, password } and no Authorization header
Verify JWT and profile are returned
cases.expected: HTTP 200; public login endpoint accepts unauthenticated requests per API design §2.6; session issued without pre-existing bearer token

cases.title: Login JWT sub claim maps to users.id UUID
preconditions: Existing account with known credentials
Ability to decode JWT sub claim
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials
Decode JWT sub from returned token
Compare sub to profile.userId and users.id in database
cases.expected: JWT sub equals users.id UUID; profile.userId matches; credential session identity aligns with roles-permissions JWT sub contract

cases.title: Login response profile includes roles array for authorization
preconditions: Existing account with known role assignment (Participant, OrganizerStaff, or OrganizerAdmin)
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials for the account
Inspect profile.roles in response body
GET /api/v1/me with returned JWT and compare role information
cases.expected: Profile roles array reflects account role rows; GET /me returns consistent role data; role information available for client route guards and API authorization per permission matrix

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin seed account login receives admin-role JWT session
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin account provisioned via seed or admin provisioning (not public signup)
Known OrganizerAdmin credentials
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with OrganizerAdmin email and password
Decode JWT role claim or GET /api/v1/me with returned token
GET /api/v1/admin/status with login JWT
cases.expected: HTTP 200 login with JWT; session role is OrganizerAdmin; admin-only protected route returns HTTP 200 confirming authenticated admin session

cases.title: Successful login enables access to sensitive protected operations
preconditions: Participant account with at least one registration
No valid session before login
steps: Confirm GET /api/v1/me/registrations without token returns HTTP 401
POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid participant credentials
GET /api/v1/me/registrations with login JWT
cases.expected: Protected endpoint blocked before login; after login returns HTTP 200 with paginated data; satisfies NFR-07 requirement that authentication precedes sensitive operations

cases.title: Invalid login attempts return standard typed error envelope with immediate feedback
preconditions: API server running
Known invalid credential combination (unknown email or wrong password)
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with invalid credentials
Parse JSON error body
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with error.code INVALID_CREDENTIALS, user-safe error.message, and requestId; no JWT or profile returned; immediate fail feedback per NFR-13 without internal stack trace or DB details

cases.title: Login response never exposes password hash or credential secrets
preconditions: Existing account with known credentials
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials
Parse full JSON response body for passwordHash, password, or hash fields
cases.expected: Response contains token and profile only; no passwordHash, password, or credential material in API response per NFR-17 password storage and exposure rules

cases.title: Login with incorrect password is rejected
preconditions: Existing account registered with known email and password
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with correct email and incorrect password
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with error.code INVALID_CREDENTIALS; no JWT returned; GET /me without valid token remains blocked

cases.title: Login with unknown email returns same invalid-credentials response as wrong password
preconditions: Email not registered in users table
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with unregistered email and any password
Compare error code and message shape to wrong-password attempt for existing account
cases.expected: HTTP 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS with generic 'Invalid email or password' message; no indication whether email exists; prevents user enumeration while blocking unauthenticated access

cases.title: validateLoginInput rejects empty password
preconditions: Auth validation module available
steps: Call validateLoginInput with valid email and empty or missing password
Observe thrown ApiError
cases.expected: ApiError with code INVALID_INPUT, statusCode 400, and details.field password; login rejected before credential lookup

cases.title: validateLoginInput rejects invalid email format
preconditions: Auth validation module available
steps: Call validateLoginInput with malformed email (missing @, empty string) and any password
Observe thrown ApiError
cases.expected: ApiError with code INVALID_INPUT, statusCode 400, and details.field email; no credential verification attempted

cases.title: Login with mixed-case email succeeds against lowercase stored account
preconditions: Account registered as user@example.com with known password
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with email User@Example.com and correct password
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JWT and profile for user@example.com; email normalized on login per same normalization rules as registration

cases.title: validateLoginInput normalizes email to lowercase trimmed form
preconditions: Auth validation module available
steps: Call validateLoginInput with email '  User@Example.com  ' and non-empty password
cases.expected: Returned email is 'user@example.com'; valid input passes validation for downstream credential lookup

cases.title: Login page preserves return URL for post-auth redirect
preconditions: Web app running with route guards
Fresh browser session with no JWT
Existing account credentials
User attempted to access protected route before login
steps: Navigate directly to a protected route while unauthenticated
Observe redirect to /login with return URL query parameter
Submit valid credentials on login form
cases.expected: After successful sign-in, user redirected to originally requested protected route per page-list route guard contract; session established without losing intended destination
